subject: [COMMON] Add pre upgradre script for network name gen
commit_message:
[COMMON] Add pre upgradre script for network name gen

Network-name-gen rolling upgrade needs to be supported to enable the
rolling upgrade of SDNC component. However, there is a value in values.yaml
when being true, changes the use of local nengdb to centralized mariadb-galera.
This involves in data migration and network name gen disruption during upgrade.
If this value is false, the new release continues to use the local nengdb and
rolling upgrade is feasible. We write a pre upgrade script to cater these two
events.

Issue-ID: OOM-2377
Change-Id: Ie3ca5d2fad940fcc8ea6e157430d4a90cd93b2b6
Signed-off-by: tringuyen <tri.nguyen@tatacommunications.com>

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

I'm a little bit puzzled here.
Could you please explain how this upgrade is going to work?
We already have upgrade for mariadb-gelera, how is it used here?
From which version to which will this upgrade happen?
Personally I dislike the fact that during the upgrade we need to use a custom job to create our DB user

Patch Set 5:

(4 comments)

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> 
> (7 comments)
> 
> I'm a little bit puzzled here.
> Could you please explain how this upgrade is going to work?
> We already have upgrade for mariadb-gelera, how is it used here?
> From which version to which will this upgrade happen?
> Personally I dislike the fact that during the upgrade we need to use a custom job to create our DB user

Hi Kryzysztof, in frankfurt, network name gen tries to store data in shared mariadb-galera instead of using local nengdb (as in El Alto). 
- This upgrade script upgrade network name gen from El Alto to Frankfurt. It first dumps nengdb from the local database nengdb to a backup file, and load this backup nengdb file to shared mariadb-galera. So the upgrade means to migrate nengdb from local database to shared database.
- When we upgrade mariadb-galera from El Alto to Frankfurt, basically data inside mariadb-galera on that time does not contain nengdb yet. So upgrade of network name gen does the job of migrating nengdb here.
- Network name gen in frankfurt must be specified with masterPassword. However, when we upgrade, we want to keep the old password from El Alto. Hence, I wrote the custom job to remove the nengdb user created from masterPassword (in Frankfurt), and create a new user using the old password from El Alto
